symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic palpitations — rapid, bounding heartbeat on standing that resolves supine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Posture-provoked, recumbency-relieved tachycardia is the cardinal feature of an orthostatic autonomic mechanism.
A re-entrant SVT is not reliably triggered by posture nor abolished by lying flat.
Catecholamine surge produces a fine postural tremor in the hyperadrenergic phenotype.
Tremor is a feature of thyrotoxicosis — non-discriminating in isolation, needs TFTs.
Tremor occurs with anxiety too; does not discriminate on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Presyncope on standing (no loss of consciousness)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic cerebral hypoperfusion / autonomic instability produces presyncope on standing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strict posture-dependency — provoked by standing, relieved by lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible provocation by upright posture and relief by recumbency is the defining discriminator of an orthostatic autonomic disorder.
A primary re-entrant tachycardia is independent of posture.
A primary panic disorder would not be reliably switched on by standing and off by lying down.
A subjective catecholamine surge is characteristic of the hyperadrenergic phenotype.
Overlaps with panic symptoms; here the surge is secondary to the physical autonomic event.
Post-exertional fatigue is commonly reported in dysautonomia; non-specific but consistent.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known chronic autonomic disorder with prior similar episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prior diagnosis of an autonomic disorder with recurrent similar episodes markedly raises the pre-test probability that this is a flare of the same condition.
A thyroid storm typically produces fever/hyperthermia; being afebrile makes a thyrotoxic crisis less likely.
Absence of chest pain reduces (but does not exclude) an ischaemic or high-risk arrhythmic process; chest pain is a mandatory escalation trigger if it appears.
Absence of frank syncope lowers the probability of a haemodynamically significant primary arrhythmia; syncope is a mandatory escalation trigger if it occurs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural haemodynamic pattern — marked HR rise and hypertensive surge on standing with normal supine values

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained orthostatic HR increment with a hypertensive (not hypotensive) BP response is characteristic of the hyperadrenergic phenotype. Formal measurement is clinician-grade; the patient's borrowed-cuff/wearable readings are supportive but unverified.
Normalisation of heart rate on lying flat is inconsistent with a sustained primary tachyarrhythmia.
A normal supine rate argues against a sustained SVT, which would persist regardless of posture.
A resting normal, regular-feeling supine rate argues against sustained AF (though paroxysmal AF still needs an ECG).

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG — sinus rhythm / posture-driven sinus tachycardia, no primary tachyarrhythmia

investigation_details.expected_result: Sinus rhythm at rest; sinus tachycardia on standing; no SVT, no atrial fibrillation, no ischaemic changes. A normal ECG does not by itself confirm POTS but excludes a primary tachyarrhythmia.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: SVT and AF cannot be formally excluded; the patient may be incorrectly reassured or incorrectly escalated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG / rhythm capture is required to formally exclude a primary re-entrant tachycardia.
An ECG is required to exclude AF.

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal thyroid function, making thyrotoxicosis unlikely as the cause of the tachycardia and tremor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thyroid function testing is required to exclude thyrotoxicosis as the driver of the sympathetic features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician examination — fine fast postural tremor, brisk symmetrical reflexes, intact cranial nerves, thyroid not enlarged

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fine fast postural tremor with brisk reflexes fits a sympathetically-driven state; these are clinician-measured findings unavailable via telehealth.
A non-enlarged thyroid slightly lowers the probability of overt thyrotoxicosis (though not diagnostic).

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is diagnostic alone, but posture-triggered tachycardia + presyncope + strict recumbency relief + a hypertensive orthostatic surge + a normal supine heart rate together are highly specific for an orthostatic autonomic mechanism and argue strongly against a primary fixed tachyarrhythmia — the pattern that should prevent over-triage.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude SVT and atrial fibrillation. Cannot be obtained via telehealth — arranged at prompt GP review; its need is a reason for in-person review, not a reason for emergency escalation of a stable patient.
Excludes thyrotoxicosis as a cause of the tachycardia, tremor and anxiety.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Supine and standing (active stand) BP and heart rate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The core diagnostic measurement for orthostatic tachycardia. Can be approximated by the patient at home with a cuff and watch, and confirmed formally by the GP.

Only if symptoms prove refractory or the pattern becomes spontaneous rather than orthostatic — screens for phaeochromocytoma. Not a first-line telehealth investigation; ordering it up-front would be over-investigation.

Not indicated: there is no chest pain and no focal neurological deficit. Reflexively ordering cardiac or neuro-imaging work-up for a posture-dependent flare with a negative red-flag screen is over-investigation.
